text:
I'm attempting to practice this kind of FPS movie style with gmod, I just have a couple of problems that perhaps someone may know how to solve? That would be great if someone could help!

So, it looks like some parts he just uses an NPC Viewcam on a citizen, then walks him over to an area, and for ragdolling he just welds a camera to a ragdolls head and goes from there. But how does he manage to get realistic movement using the ragdoll? And also, how does he manage to get the NPC to not look around randomly and pay attention to areas he wants the Viewcam to look at? And how does he display the moving hands while running and such. Is this just greenscreen layers over a recording of running or something?

One more thing. Some parts look like they were filmed just through the player's camera, but how does he get the camera to bob or shake a little for realistic walking? Is there some sort of addon I can download for this?

EDIT I found an addon called SharpYe that simulates realistic head bobbing and motions, so that helps.
author: @HoovyMcBoovy

text: Well i mean, for sure but, that still doesnt answer my question.
author: @HoovyMcBoovy

text:
Pardon if this is a topic of discontinued interest, but I have some answers that may help, as I also like to pursue this kind of style.
The realistic movement, if you're talking about the first person things, are just green screen recordings he must've made, then slapped it on top of the scenes. For the gestures and other things like rolls, I assume he may have used either a barrel and welded the ragdoll to it with no-collide, or some awesome trick, for other things, he may have used a sort of pose-to-pose addon, like Henry's Animation Tool, although I don't recall how old it is.
With the NPCs Usually, in a certain npc movement addon, citizens look around, however rebels usually don't. Plus, if they're moving somewhere or doing something, their head just points forward.
Hope I helped a little.
